RawComment: How in-depth the studies are and how planned the teachers are to teach the subjects, most are prepared with what they are going to teach and you can see their passion in the way they teach. There needs to be a standardised template for powerpoints and slides and also how they design moodle and where they place items. I have found that each course has different structures and settings and i am not sure if there has been a role out of powerpoints and slides that the teachers should be using?.
Knowledge / passion of teachers. The foreigner: australian student ratio is way too drastic. As an australian, I feel like I am studying in another country.

I have completed some fascinating and engaging courses over the years that had some great teachers! I only completed one course this year which was just fine!. UNSW needs to fund its Arts Departments/ Degree. This course suffers from a lack of options and a very under resourced and underfunded staff. I think there is a lot of potential but it's clear that Arts is not valued at UNSW which is disappointing.

The flexibility of being able to pick elective subjects that align with my interests. Accessible resources and textbooks need to be more available. Having classes offered in more than one term. Trimesters not allowing for a depth of understanding a subject - it's too quick and feels like we just scratch the surface of every topic.
Going in placement and learning about the different disciplines of medicine. More practice exams for the biomed final at the end of this year.

Probably the tutorials and the interactive components where groups are able to share idea and learn off each other. The cost. The cost of classes has gone up significantly, especially during covid where the capacity to learn was greatly diminished.
